workflow.name: Bridge iMessage and Slack threads with Blooio for two-way messaging
workflow.description:
Turn any Slack channel into your team's iMessage inbox. 

## How it works

📱 → Customer texts your business number
🔵 → Blooio pushes the webhook into n8n
💬 → n8n posts a new thread in Slack
🧵 → Your team replies in the thread
🔵 → n8n sends the reply through Blooio
📱 → Customer gets a blue-bubble iMessage

## Why this matters

| What you get | Why it matters |
|--------------|----------------|
| 💙 Blue bubbles, not green | iMessage open rates blow past SMS. People read texts from friends — not from 10DLC short codes.  |
| 🧰 One tech stack, not five | No separate texting app, no SMS gateway, no CRM seat for a receptionist. Just Slack + Blooio. Your team already knows Slack. |
| 💼 Turn Slack into a mini iMessage CRM | Every contact = one thread. Scroll the channel, see every active conversation. Search by phone, name, or keyword. Your history lives where your team already works. |
| 📱 iMessage from any device | Desktop, phone, tablet, browser — anywhere Slack runs, your team can text customers. No shared iPhone sitting on a desk. No "let me check when I'm at my computer." |
| 👥 Team inbox + personal reach | Your whole team sees inbound messages and can jump in. But replies still come from your business number — not from whoever happens to be on shift. Customer experience stays consistent; coverage doesn't. |

***More opens = more leads = more deals.***


## What you need

| Tool | Cost | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| Blooio | ~$39/mo starting | Send + receive iMessages via REST API |
| Slack | Any plan | Dedicated bridge channel (private recommended) |
| n8n | Cloud or self-hosted | Run the workflow |

⚠️ **Blooio P2P notice:** Blooio requires all outreach to be opt-in and actively enforces this.  

## The two branches

| Branch | Trigger | What it does |
|--------|---------|--------------|
| 🟥 Inbound iMessage → Slack | Blooio webhook (message.received) | Post new iMessage to Slack as a threaded parent |
| 🟩 Slack Reply → Outbound iMessage | Any new message in bridge channel | Filter → get parent → extract phone → send via Blooio |

## Set up steps

- Takes about 15 minutes
- Sign up at blooio.com, grab your API token and webhook  
- Create a dedicated Slack channel 
- Connect Slack credentials
- Create an HTTP Bearer Auth credential with your Blooio API token 
- Copy the Production URL to "Blooio Webhook" 
- Text yourself to confirm it posts threaded in Slack, reply in the thread, confirm blue bubble arrived.

### More where this came from →[Orchestrate Academy](https://orchestrate.academy)

channelId.value: <__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Same Slack channel ID as above — e.g. C08EF1BE8T1__>

parameters.content:
## 📖 README: iMessage ↔ Slack Bridge

**What This Does**
A complete 2-branch bidirectional bridge that turns one Slack channel into a full business iMessage inbox. Inbound iMessages post as new parent messages. Reply in-thread, the reply goes back as iMessage to the original number. One channel, unlimited conversations, zero state store.

**The Two Branches:**

**Branch 1: Inbound iMessage → Slack** (Blooio Webhook)
- Blooio webhook fires the moment an iMessage arrives at your business number
- IF node filters to message.received events only (ignores delivery, read, sent receipts)
- Slack node posts a new parent message with the sender's phone embedded in the text
- Your team now has a threaded inbox they can reply to

**Branch 2: Slack Reply → Outbound iMessage** (Slack Trigger)
- Slack Trigger listens 24/7 for new messages in the bridge channel
- Filter node drops self-posts (bot_message, bot_id) and non-threaded messages
- Set node regexes the phone number out of the parent text
- HTTP Request sends the reply as iMessage via Blooio — blue bubble delivered

**Who This Is For**
- Agencies triaging client iMessages
- Law firms and real estate agents routing inbound texts
- Dental and medical practices wanting blue-bubble premium feel
- Solo operators keeping personal iPhone separate from work
- Customer support teams replacing established brands at 1/10th the cost

**What You Need**
- Blooio account + warmed iMessage number (~$39/mo starting)
- **!! NOTICE !!** Blooio is a P2P service — all iMessages must be opt-in. Perfect for replying to inbound traffic. 
- Slack workspace + a dedicated bridge channel (private recommended)
- n8n (cloud or self-hosted)

**Setup Steps**

1. **Blooio Setup**
   - Sign up at blooio.com and activate a warmed iMessage number
   - Generate an API token from the dashboard
   - Keep the webhook signing secret handy for HMAC verification

2. **Slack Setup**
   - Create a dedicated bridge channel (private recommended)
   - Create a Slack app or use the built-in n8n OAuth
   - Invite the bot to the bridge channel
   - Grant scopes: channels:history, groups:history, chat:write, channels:read

3. **Branch 1: Inbound iMessage → Slack**
   - Open Blooio Webhook node and copy the Production URL
   - In Blooio dashboard, register this URL and subscribe to message.received
   - Connect your Slack credential on Post iMessage to Slack
   - Select your bridge channel

4. **Branch 2: Slack Reply → Outbound iMessage**
   - Connect your Slack credential on Slack Thread Reply
   - Select the SAME bridge channel as Branch 1 — they must match
   - Connect your Slack credential on Get Thread Parent
   - Create an HTTP Bearer Auth credential with your Blooio API token
   - Attach it to Send iMessage via Blooio

5. **Test & Activate**
   - Text your Blooio number from your own phone
   - Confirm it posts to the bridge channel as a new threaded message
   - Reply in the thread from Slack
   - Confirm the reply arrives as iMessage on your phone
   - Toggle workflow Active

**Self-Loop Protection**
The Filter node drops any message where bot_id exists or subtype equals bot_message. This prevents the bot from replying to its own posts — a critical safeguard when the trigger fires on ANY channel message.


**HMAC Signature Verification (Production)**
For production, verify that incoming webhooks actually came from Blooio  (+1 code node) https://docs.blooio.com/guides/webhook-signatures


**Troubleshooting**
- Nothing posts to Slack → Blooio webhook not registered, or IF node filtered the event. Check $json.body.event value
- Bot replies to itself → Filter not catching; inspect the Slack trigger payload for bot_id and subtype
- Phone regex misses → Parent was edited and no longer contains +XXXXXXXXXXX
- iMessage not sending → Verify Blooio token, check capability at /v1/api/contacts/{phone}/capabilities

---


## Learn more at [Orchestrate Academy](https://www.orchestrate.academy)

## 🎯 Branch 1: Inbound iMessage → Slack

**Trigger:** Blooio Webhook (message.received event)

**What It Does:**
1. Receives the webhook the moment an iMessage arrives at your business number
2. Filters to message.received only — drops delivery, read, sent, reaction receipts
3. Posts to your Slack bridge channel as a new parent message
4. Embeds the sender's phone in the message text — this IS the routing key

**Data Posted to Slack:**
- Sender phone number (formatted as `+1XXXXXXXXXX` in a code span)
- The full iMessage body
- A hint asking the team to reply in thread

**Key Point:** The phone number embedded in the Slack parent message is how Branch 2 knows who to reply to. No database. No lookup table. The thread IS the routing table.

## 💬 Branch 2: Slack Reply → Outbound iMessage

**Trigger:** Any new message in the Slack bridge channel

**What It Does:**
1. Listens 24/7 for new messages in the bridge channel
2. Filter drops self-posts (bot_message, bot_id) and non-threaded messages — guards against loops
3. Get Thread Parent fetches the original iMessage post via Slack's replies API
4. Set node regexes the phone number from the parent text
5. HTTP Request sends the team's reply as iMessage via Blooio

**Why The Filter Matters:**
When the Slack bot posts to the bridge channel, that post would itself fire the trigger — creating an infinite loop. The 4-condition filter (has thread_ts, not parent, no bot_id, no bot_message subtype) is the safety mechanism.

**Key Point:** This is a real bidirectional bridge. Your team works in Slack like they always do. Customers get blue bubbles on their iPhone. Nobody has to learn a new inbox.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
